title: Model Lab Intermediate Cluster Split Level
old_clustering_issue: The previous Model Lab overlay had one clustered stage between countywide aggregation and parcel detail. At medium-close zoom, large grouped bubbles could stay fused too long, so a high-count cluster could look like one giant marker instead of splitting into nearby sub-clusters.

new_four_stage_clustering_behavior.stage_a_countywide_fused_clusters: Scale >= 78000 renders countywide fused clusters using broad 0.08-degree cells and capped cell count for strategic overview.
new_four_stage_clustering_behavior.stage_b_intermediate_subclusters: Scale >= 36000 renders intermediate_subclusters using the existing 0.038-degree grouping so broad countywide clusters split into readable sub-clusters before parcel detail.
new_four_stage_clustering_behavior.stage_c_fine_local_clusters: Scale >= 9000 renders fine_local_clusters using smaller 0.014-degree cells and a higher cap so medium-close views split oversized groups into local clusters instead of one giant bubble.
new_four_stage_clustering_behavior.stage_d_individual_markers: Scale < 9000 renders parcel-safe individual research markers capped for readability.

clustering_radius_changes.implementation: SceneViewContainer now uses getModelResearchClusterCellSize() and getModelResearchClusterMaxCells() so radius and cap vary by display stage.

sizing_behavior_preserved.approach: Existing count-bucket sizing remains intact, with circle size still driven by represented record count.
sizing_behavior_preserved.fine_cluster_adjustment: Fine local clusters use a modest 0.9 size multiplier and reduced world-length halos so local split clusters do not visually merge back into one large bubble.

count_label_behavior.clusters: Grouped clusters with count > 1 keep centered count labels.
count_label_behavior.fine_local_clusters: Fine local clusters keep count labels when grouped.
count_label_behavior.individual_markers: Parcel detail markers do not show cluster count labels.

selected_cluster_behavior.behavior: Clicked aggregate graphics continue to emit safe ModelResearchPreviewMarker context with contextKind=cluster or heatmap_cell, representedFeatureCount, bandCounts, dominantResearchBand, top drivers, model version, data quality flag, and internal-only caveat.
selected_cluster_behavior.grammar_guardrail: Existing right-panel grammar for single features is preserved: '1 parcel represented', not '1 parcels represented'.

area_label_behavior.implementation: Clusters now attempt to reuse a safe existing approximateAreaLabel from member markers when available. If the source label is not usable, the UI falls back to neutral count-based labels.
fallbacks: Research surface cell of N parcels
area_label_behavior.anti_fake_data_guardrail: No road, municipality, subdivision, or neighborhood names are invented. Context labels are only reused from available marker context.

performance_notes: Countywide and medium views do not render all individual records.
Fine local clustering delays individual markers until closest zoom.
Rendered aggregate counts remain capped by stage.
Cluster helpers keep aggregation choices stage-specific without adding external dependencies.

snapshot_report_integration.status: Existing Model Lab snapshot/report fields continue to preserve selected cluster or marker context.
snapshot_report_integration.report_caveat: This Model Lab snapshot summarizes a relative research cluster. It does not show exact parcel development probability and is not an official parcel score.

safety_verification.safety_scan: rg found existing internal schema/service guardrail fields such as prediction_probability_available=false and exact_probability_available=false, plus existing internal development_signal_class repository usage. No new Phase 23J UI exposure of exact probabilities, raw scores, or official parcel prediction classes was added.

smoke_test_results.browser_console: No blocking app errors observed. ArcGIS labelPlacement warnings appeared and are consistent with known external ArcGIS warnings.
smoke_test_results.visual_medium_close_split_verified: Not conclusively verified in browser automation; static thresholds/tests verify the new intermediate/fine split logic.

remaining_limitations: Browser smoke confirmed countywide fused clusters but did not visually verify the specific C.C. Griffin medium-close 79-record cluster split.
Area labels only use available marker context or neutral fallbacks; no new geographic naming data was added.
Scale thresholds may still need visual tuning after hands-on map QA across multiple county extents.
